human_verification.notes: Reviewed issue #1286 source baseline, boundary discipline, scenario coverage, functionality mapping, and deployment/commercialization notes for distribution execution readiness and escalation behavior.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to distribution execution support, exception handling, completion evidence capture, and adjacent-lane return rules for switching/safety/legal/procurement-adjacent actions.
Authority sources in `manifest.yaml` map to named power-industry governance, distribution terminology, outage-readiness workflows, and evidence-control posture with review cadence and decay policy.

Evaluation scenarios (dop-01 to dop-10) cover normal execution, evidence gaps, conflicting records, boundary refusal, critical-load semantics, shortage escalation, adjacent-lane routing, low-confidence escalation, and rework-completion.
Functionality map declares all shared, workflow-phase, and industry-profile abilities required by the queue and maps each to passing scenario IDs with required inputs, outputs, and refusal triggers.
delegated_meta_agent_evidence: Research quality and commercialization wording may be refreshed through shared meta-agent support paths when available, without weakening the local baseline or skipping hard refusal/evidence gates.
Readiness evidence structure is validated by this package and local repo checks; no additional delegated contract is required for deployment readiness claims.
notes: This evidence package supports validated claim for a specialist overlay that remains runtime-strategy `specification-based` until a retained implementation is separately promoted.
No unreviewed regression was identified in the local spec pack structure at generation time.
